q: Nerve impulse propagation: What is the correct sequence of ionic events during an action potential?
options: K+ enters → membrane depolarizes → Na+ exits → repolarization occurs
Na+ channels open → Na+ influx → depolarization → K+ channels open → K+ efflux → repolarization → Na+/K+ pump restores gradient
Cl- exits → hyperpolarization → Na+ enters slowly → slow depolarization
Ca2+ enters axon → triggers Na+ release from vesicles → membrane potential changes
explanation: Action potential: voltage-gated Na+ channels open (depolarization) → peak at ~+40mV → Na+ channels close, K+ channels open (repolarization) → brief hyperpolarization → Na+/K+ pump restores resting potential.
